events.label: assistant
events.text: Assessment written to `/app/answer.json`: **fail**, high risk. The impulse taps are reversed for their service — gas tap on the bottom (should be top, sloping continuously down to the transmitter so condensate drains back to process) and liquid tap on top (should be side/bottom, sloping continuously up so entrained gas migrates back to process). This classic reversal traps the wrong phase in each leg, causing plugging, gas-locking, and inaccurate/unstable DP readings. Since the work is only rough-complete, it's correctable now via re-tapping and re-routing before fill/leak-test/calibration — the quality gate should hold until reworked and re-inspected per API RP 551 and the transmitter's manufacturer manual.
